decision: Refuse to invent a repository command or raw storage-key operation.
nextStep: Open an incident, identify the exact target through provider readbacks, obtain one-operation authority, execute one provider-owned bounded containment or revocation, and read back the postcondition.
evidenceRequired: Incident record and exact deployment, config, alias, subject, store, and cipher identity.
Authenticated Vercel and Upstash metadata plus sanitized health, auth, and profile result.
One-operation authority, rollback, revocation receipt, and before/after provider readback.
limitations: The repository exposes no universal revoke, delete, rollback, or rotation command.
nonClaims: No revocation is complete without a provider-owned operation and postcondition readback.
rollbackRevocation: Reverse only the recorded change; Vercel, Upstash, the secret store, and the Codex account owner each control their own state. Preserve the newest fenced profile generation.
unsafeAmbiguities: The compromised bearer and exact target are unknown, so only incident opening and a stop on consequential work are safe before readback.
